summary: Xbox One controller connected via Bluetooth has wrong mappings during gameplay on Linux (similar to #101215)

description.text:
### Tested versions

I don't have the version numbers, but two games I tested are Parking Garage Rally Circuit and Delta V: Rings of Saturn. I will be happy to update this issue if someone can tell me how to open the debug menu in those games. I tried the F3 key and nothing came up.

### System information

OpenSUSE Leap 15.6 (Linux kernel 6.4.0), AMD Ryzen 4500U with Radeon graphics, glxinfo -    Vendor: AMD (0x1002)     Device: AMD Radeon Graphics (radeonsi, renoir, LLVM 17.0.6, DRM 3.57, 6.4.0-150600.23.38-default) (0x1636)     Version: 23.3.4

### Issue description

Controller USB ID: ID `045e:02ea` Microsoft Corp. Xbox One Controller

```
me@my-machine ~ $ bluetoothctl info
Device C8:3F:26:XX:XX:XX (public)
	Name: Xbox Wireless Controller
	Alias: Xbox Wireless Controller
	Appearance: 0x03c4 (964)
	Icon: input-gaming
	Paired: yes
	Bonded: yes
	Trusted: yes
	Blocked: no
	Connected: yes
	LegacyPairing: no
	UUID: Vendor specific           (00000001-5f60-4c4f-9c83-a7953298d40d)
	UUID: Generic Access Profile    (00001800-0000-1000-8000-00805f9b34fb)
	UUID: Generic Attribute Profile (00001801-0000-1000-8000-00805f9b34fb)
	UUID: Device Information        (0000180a-0000-1000-8000-00805f9b34fb)
	UUID: Battery Service           (0000180f-0000-1000-8000-00805f9b34fb)
	UUID: Human Interface Device    (00001812-0000-1000-8000-00805f9b34fb)
	Modalias: usb:v045Ep0B20d0522
	Battery Percentage: 0x64 (100)
```

When booting a Godot game such as Parking Garage Rally Circuit, the button mappings for an Xbox One controller connected via Bluetooth are correct in text menus, but are incorrect during gameplay. This behavior is similar to or may be related to issue #101215. I've also encountered this issue in Delta V: Rings of Saturn, which made me believe this is a bug in Godot itself rather than in just one of the games.

I have no issues with the Xbox controller in wireless mode in other Linux applications such as Steam Big Picture. I thought this issue was different enough to warrant opening a new one rather than just piggybacking on #101215.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Connect a Xbox One controller via Bluetooth
2. Launch a Godot game such as Parking Garage Rally Circuit on Linux
3. Observe that the labeled buttons correspond to the real buttons, at least in the menu
4. Once in game, observe that the button mapping has changed to swap buttons around

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I'm not a Godot dev. If this isn't the appropriate place to post this, I'm sorry.

summary: Accidental touch on top left trackpad corner sometimes crashes editor window

description.text:
### Tested versions

Happens on Godot v4.3.stable - macOS 14.6.1 - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Pro - Apple M1 Pro (10 Threads)
Never happened when using 4.2 stable.

### System information

Macbook Pro 16" - macOS 14.6.1 - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Pro - Apple M1 Pro (10 Threads)

### Issue description

I have large hands, and occasionally while typing code in Godot the base of my left thumb will occasionally touch the top left trackpad corner of my macbook pro. Sometimes (not every time, it seems to happen randomly) this touch will do two things:
1. Cause the main editor window to turn black. No swiping animation happens, the window just goes black. I've only observed this with fullscreen enabled. 
2. A Godot editor file search window I've never seen before pops up over the black window

If I'm able to stop myself typing as soon as the file search window appears, I can either click on the file search window or perform a swiping gesture on the trackpad to bring the editor window back into focus. But if I hit a key after the file search window appears, the file search window will go fullscreen, at which point its almost impossible to get the Godot editor to reappear. If I swipe up to see the open windows tray, the Godot window is blacked out. There is no indication in the bottom system tray that the window is minimized. Sometimes by using the "Window" system tab at the top of the screen and fiddling with minimize, tile window to left of screen, etc, I can get the window to reappear when maximizing the window, but its not reliable. Usually I'll end up force quitting Godot.

I really do apologize for how weird and random this bug is, but I've scoured my system settings, and as far as I'm aware I don't have a trackpad gesture set up that would react to a top left touch. This also never happens outside using Godot. Hopefully its something like a hotkey setting that I just need to change, but this has happened to be regularly enough (and caused me to lose enough work) that I thought it was time to submit a bug report. 

Thanks in advance for taking the time, and thanks for what you do to enable developers. 

### Steps to reproduce

While typing in the code editor window, an accidental press of the very top left corner of the Macbook trackpad. It feels like it may trigger if the touch happens at the same time as a keypress, but I've not been able to confirm this yet.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I've included my editor settings .tres in case it proves helpful

[EditorSettings.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18995938/EditorSettings.zip)

summary: Joystick: small changes near zero trigger repeated action release events

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.3
4.4.beta1

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta1 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon RX 6800 (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12033.1030) - AMD Ryzen 5 7600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

I have a `func _input(event : InputEvent):` function that is repeatedly receiving `event.is_action_released("ui_up")` etc. from a joystick that is nearly centered but producing slightly jittery output. This is preventing me from tracking whether the action is currently genuinely logically pressed/released, e.g. the joystick's jitter near 0 is making my code think that dpad buttons are being released.

I'm trying to write an autoload that automatically, smartly produces virtual echo InputEvents for dpad/controller UI navigation inputs. It works except for this one godot bug. When I hold dpad up/down and the left analog stick is jittering on the y axis, it fails to produce echo commands because of false release events from the joystick.

Workaround: track analog sticks separately from all other inputs, duplicating a LOT of code.

What Godot should do instead: only have joystick/analog inputs produce press/release events when crossing from one side of the deadzone threshold to the other.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3dc2b6f3-f943-4cbb-ad66-4401d8f3b0aa

### Steps to reproduce

Create a `func _input(event : InputEvent):` that prints joystick events and watch it produce lots of release events when moving the joystick near 0, or replicate the attached video with the attached reproduction project.

"When I hold dpad up/down and the left analog stick is jittering on the y axis, it fails to produce echo commands because of false release events from the joystick."

summary: Exported array or dictionary in a preloaded packed scene that is modified at runtime retains changes even after changing scenes

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.3

### System information

macOS 15.3 + Windows 11

### Issue description

Exported arrays and dictionaries in a preloaded packed scene that is instantiated and whose content is changed at runtime will keep these modified changes even after changing scenes. Instantiating this packed scene in another scene will show the arrays and dictionaries retaining the changes instead of reseting to the original exported values.

This ONLY happens when the exported arrays and dictionaries have values changed in the editor prior to runtime and not using the default values. It suggests that the packed scene is always cached in memory (due to the preload?) and the changed exported arrays and dictionaries are still passed by reference. It also ONLY happens when the packed scene is preloaded.

This is possibly related to other open issues regarding arrays and dictionaries during scene changes or resets. It could be the expected behavior after all and I am misunderstanding how preloads work.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP.
2. Run the "Test1A" scene (it is the main scene). This scene contains two variables tmp_array and tmp_dictionary that are empty at load. All instructions can be read on screen.
3. Press Up to display what is in these variables at any time via the Result text.
4. Press Left to instantiate a constant preloaded packed scene "TestPacked" containing two exported variables some_array and some_dictionary with editor modified values of [3, 2, 1] and {"key1": 6, "key2": 5, "key3": 4} respectively, and assign these to tmp_array and tmp_dictionary
5. Press Up to check that the assignment completed. It should show those values above.
6. Press Down to change tmp_array and tmp_dictionary to [0, 0, 0] and {"key1": 0, "key2": 0, "key3": 0} respectively.
7. Press Right to change the scene to "Test1B".
8. In Test 1B, press right to change back to "Test1A".
9. Press Left to instantiate "TestPacked" and assign the values to tmp_array and tmp_dictionary again.
10. Press Up to check the values. I expected them to be the original [3, 2, 1] and {"key1": 6, "key2": 5, "key3": 4} but it is the modified [0, 0, 0] and {"key1": 0, "key2": 0, "key3": 0} instead.

If you open TestPacked and reset the edited exported array and dictionary to the default values in editor and run this test again, then the values will reset to the defaults after modifying and changing scenes, which is the behavior that is expected.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[PackedScene_Persistence.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18980458/PackedScene_Persistence.zip)

summary: `GDScriptLanguageProtocol` not initialized if error in workspace

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.rc1

### System information

Windows 10, v4.4.rc1

### Issue description

I am using the VSCode plugin for Godot. When editing a GDScript file with a `class_name` ,while godot and as such the LSP is not running, such that there is an error in the file, `GDScriptLanguageProtocol::initialize` will not be called once godot is restarted and VSCode connects to the LSP. This results in `workspace->root_uri` being empty which then results in
```
error while getting cache for script "file:res:/res:/res:/e:res:/Devres:/gw2cc_publicres:/libccres:/resourcesres:/CharacterState.gd"

<modules\gdscript\gdscript.cpp(1115): GDScript::load_source_code> Condition "err" is true. Returning: err:
Attempt to open script 'file:res:/res:/res:/e:res:/Devres:/gw2cc_publicres:/libccres:/resourcesres:/CharacterState.gd' resulted in error 'File not found'.
```

and
```
Class "CharacterState" hides a global script class.
```

Fixing the error and restarting VSCode will fix the issue.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a custom resource with a `class_name` and add some export properties with setters. Close godot and edit the file in VScode to produce an error. Restart godot. Get flooded with errors.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

```
extends Resource
class_name CharacterState

@export var profession: int: set = set_professio
var _hash: int = 0

func set_profession(value: int) -> void:
	_hash = 0
	profession = value
```

summary: Fix custom cursor flickering in web builds

description.text:
Fixes #102403.

Implement caching of the custom cursor state in `DisplayServerWeb` to prevent redundant updates to the browser's cursor. This change computes a hash based on the cursor resource, shape, and hotspot, and only updates the browser's cursor when the hash changes. This mitigates the flickering observed in web exports when `set_custom_mouse_cursor()` is called every frame.

summary: Compress all binary resources on import when the editor setting is enabled

description.text:
- Follow-up to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/103284
(can be merged independently).

The **Compress Binary Resources** editor setting (which is enabled by default) was previously used in a few resource types, but it was missing in a lot of places, which reduced its potential.

This adds it to all resource types that are imported as binary. This reduces the `.godot/` folder and exported PCK size. (ZIPs are mostly unaffected because they're already compressed, but PCKs themselves are not compressed.)

The lightmap UV unwrap cache generated on import is now also compressed. On Truck Town, this reduces its file size from 661 KB down to 330 KB.

Remember to remove `.godot/` in your project then reopen the editor to fully benefit from these changes.

Platformer 3D PCK size: 2,789,744 -> **2,529,024 bytes** (-261 KB, 1.1x smaller)

- This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11847.

summary: [Web] `DisplayServer.tts_get_voices` returns an empty array on the first load

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.4.beta3

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta3 - Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon Graphics (radeonsi, renoir, LLVM 19.1.1, DRM 3.57, 6.8.0-53-lowlatency) - AMD Ryzen 9 4900HS with Radeon Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

In a web export, `DisplayServer.tts_get_voices` returns an empty array on the first load (in the `_ready` function of an autoload).

summary: MultiMesh Stride width for TRANSFORM_2D incorrect

description.text:
### Tested versions

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot 4.4-rc1

### Issue description

A change a couple weeks back to scene/resources/multimesh.cpp in Commit e1af7b65b28087f9251eda03a3327e1313c9b51d has introduced a calculation for the stride width in the PackedFloat32Array.

However, I believe the 2D size of "8" floats is a bug; a Transform2D is only 6 floats (3 columns of Vector2).

This seemingly leads to uninitialized or corrupted memory (visible in editor when setting a non-zero size),
and also at runtime presenting considerable difficulty setting these buffers from C# (e.g. from an `Transform2D[ ]` array marshalled to `float[ ]`).

summary: Animation retargeting for non-humanoid skeletons doesn't work

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.26100 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon(TM) RX 7700S (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 31.0.24018.2001) - AMD Ryzen 9 7940HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 Threads)

### Issue description

Attached is a Godot project with the issue illustrated between characters that use the Skeleton Humanoid profile versus characters that use exported skeleton profiles from their respective skeletons. In general, the animations were created for character A, being retargeted to character B's skeleton. Character B has longer legs and arms.

There are two animations, one where the position values of the bones are not keyed (except the hips) and one where the position values are keyed. For the humanoid characters, you can see that the retargeting does a somewhat good job (although there's issues where the legs look a little skewed). You can tell that the position values in the animation are relative (i.e. the hip position first keyframe is "1", even though if you check the bone position in both characters, they have different values).

However, if you look to the nonhumanoid characters, you can clearly see the hip animation position value is the same as the actual bone position in the hierarchy, for *both* characters a and b. This results in what looks like character B's bones snapping to the character A's bones.

Perhaps a step is being missed in setting up the bonemap/skeleton profile where something more manual has to be done for non-humanoids.

### Steps to reproduce

In the attached minimal reproduction project (MRP):
- Open char_a_nonhumanoid.tscn and char_b_nonhumanoid.tscn
- Toggle "Show Rest Only" on the respective GeneralSkeleton's to compare how different the characters are
- Play the animations on each character and compare the proportions. You will notice they are generally the same because the bones on char_b have been "snapped" to the absolute positions defined in the animations.
- Look at the values in a frame in the animation player for char_a_nonhumanoid vs char_b_nonhumanoid. You will notice the values are absolute, the same for both characters regardless of the rest poses being different.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[anim-retargeting.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18949204/anim-retargeting.zip)

summary: No audio when changing resolution and window scale

description.text:
### Tested versions

This bug is reproducible in both the latest stable version (4.3-stable) and the latest release candidate (4.4-rc1).

### System information

Windows 11 - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop - Godot v4.3-stable - Vulkan(Forward+)

### Issue description

I just added the option to rescale the interface to my game when I notice that setting the minimum scale causes the sound to disappear.

After much research, I have discovered the conditions for this puzzling bug to occur.

The resolution must be set to a very different value than the default (1920x1080 in my case) and the scale must be set to minimum (0.5). Both conditions must be met at the same time.

I have to say that this bug can easily be one of the strangest I have ever encountered in my life. My limited mind cannot understand what kind of relationship there is between the window and the audio that causes this.

I hope it has a solution, otherwise I will not be able to support players who have a lower resolution than the average.

2. Load a stream and set the autoplay property to true

6. Run the scene and verify that there is no sound

summary: The list of global groups don't always autocomplete when using group related methods

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.4 RC 1

### System information

Godot v4.4.rc1 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6117) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900HX (32 threads)

### Issue description

I was wondering if the situation below is intended behavior for the Godot script editor. The list of available global groups only appears in any of the group related methods for a node which is referenced using `$` or `%` notation.

This problem seems related to godotengine/godot#99277 and this summary (https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/86172#issuecomment-1860646328) of autocomplete issues in Godot. 

I can understand not wanting to give incorrect options for an unknown node in the case of node.get_node() or animations in the case of animation_player.play(). However, no matter what node (given that it has been typed or casted as a Node) you are calling `is_in_group()` on, providing the list of global groups shouldn't do any harm.

### Steps to reproduce

I've provided several examples of where the autocomplete works in the Godot Script editor, and where it does not.

```gdscript
extends Node2D

@onready var test_node: Node = Node.new() as Node
@onready var control: Control = $Control
@onready var control_1: Control = %Control

func _ready() -> void:
	
	is_in_group("globalTestGroup") # Gives list of options
	get_tree().get_nodes_in_group("globalTestGroup") # Gives list of options
	control.is_in_group("globalTestGroup") # Gives list of options
	control_1.is_in_group("globalTestGroup") # Gives list of options
	
	var this_node: Node = self as Node
	
	self.is_in_group("globalTestGroup") # Doesn't give list of options
	this_node.is_in_group("globalTestGroup") # Doesn't give list of options
	Node.new().is_in_group("globalTestGroup") # Doesn't give list of options
	test_node.is_in_group("globalTestGroup") # Doesn't give list of options

func test_func(passed_node: Node):
	passed_node.is_in_group("globalTestGroup") # Doesn't give list of options
	
	if passed_node is Node:
		passed_node.is_in_group("globalTestGroup") # Doesn't give list of options

```

N/A

summary: Add "Go Online" button on Export Template Manager

description.text:
Resolves godotengine/godot-proposals#11834

The code follows the one-click method of fixing the required ETC2/ASTC texture compression for Android export, with the same LinkButton that enables a project settings item (in this case, network mode) when clicked, and refresh the popup window (in this case, the Export Template Manager) afterwards. The addition is small enough and sufficient to be added to 4.4 before the stable version launches, since the main purpose of this feature is to make it less confusing for beginners to download export template with the new network mode that defaults to offline.

The label is written that way to avoid repeating the already established 'offline mode' on the mirrors OptionButton above it. It was placed below the "Install from File" button (instead on the same HboxContainer as it) to prevent them colliding and mess with the UI, especially with editors with bigger display scale. (Feel free to move it upward if that wouldn't be the case)

summary: Awaiting AcceptDialog.visibility_changed causes error but works fine

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.4.rc1.official [8ed125b42], v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]

### System information

Windows 11 - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility)

### Issue description

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6a95f565-6fc6-4cf4-b51e-9cacee90f0a2)

The idea is "user action required before continuing".

`AcceptDialog` and `ConfirmationDialog` do not have a signal that you can receive when the user does _anything_ (either confirms, closes, or presses a different button you added). It will, however, hide the window and emit the `visibility_changed` signal whenever you press anything.

This approach works as intended. The `Window`s appear in order, and interacting with them does what it's supposed to. It will, however, print an error every time you call `show()` or `popup()`, with the exception of the first one.

### Workaround

You can attach a script to the `Window` in question and define your own signal:
```
extends AcceptDialog

signal user_acted


func _ready() -> void:
	canceled.connect(func() -> void:
		user_acted.emit())
	confirmed.connect(func() -> void:
		user_acted.emit())
```
This half works, as whenever the `canceled` signal is triggered (when pressing the button or closing the window), the `Window` will not be visible on the next loop iteration. This is strange because the `confirmed` signal works correctly. You can fix this by emitting the custom signal deferred.

I am unsure what the problem is here, as one would imagine the process to be straightforward:
1. User interacts with `Window`
2. `Window` emits either `confirmed` or `canceled` signal.
3. `Window` is hidden (either here or before step 2).
4. Custom signal is emitted. Window is, at this point, hidden.
5. Loop receives the signal and continues iterating.

### Other notes

I have tried, unsuccessfully, different flag combinations and settings for `ConfirmationDialog` and `AcceptDialog`, as well as manually `hide()`ing them. I've also tried using the `close_requested` signal, but it doesn't emit when pressing the Cancel Button, only when closing the `Window`. The `get_cancel_button().pressed`, on the other hand, doesn't emit when closing the `Window`.

Given that using `visibility_changed` works as intended but prints errors, and I couldn't find anything about this online or in the docs, I am unsure if this is a bug or I'm doing something wrong.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Download MRP. It contains four different functions that do the above using both a `custom signal` and `visibility_changed`.
2. Run the function you wish to test in `_ready()`

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[confirmation-dialog-error-4.4.rc1.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18931931/confirmation-dialog-error-4.4.rc1.zip)
